title: Community Development Manager Valerie Chadwick Appointed as Chair of Governor’s  Council on Missing and Murdered Indigenous Persons
summary: Congratulations to our own Community Development Manager Valerie Chadwick, originally from the Algaaciq Tribe in Saint Mary’s, for her appointment as the chair of the Governor’s Council on Missing and Murdered Indigenous Persons. title: BSNC Shareholder Ayyu Qassataq Shares Master’s Thesis
summary: BSNC shareholder Ayyu Qassataq’s University of Alaska Fairbanks master’s thesis titled “When Uŋalaqłiq Danced: Stories of Strength, Suppression & Hope” explores events in Uŋalaqłiq (Unalakleet) around the turn of the 20th century from an Inuk perspective. title: Career Pathways Series – Health: Ashley Sockpick
summary: Ashley Sockpick with her daughter and dad Ricky Sockpick. BSNC’s Career Pathways Series helps showcase jobs held by successful shareholders and descendants. This series aims to raise awareness of these career options and the essential skills needed to succeed in these positions. title: When it doesn’t rain enough in the rainforest￼
summary: In 2018, Ketchikan, Alaska, received 100 inches of rain. It was deep in a drought. Compare that to Las Vegas, with its measly four inches of annual rainfall, or California’s Central Valley, with 20 inches. It’s hard to imagine that 100 inches of rain could cause concern. Yet, from 2016-2019, Southeast Alaska experienced drinking water shortages, insect outbreaks, low hydroelectric output and more wil...

summary: Battle Dawgs is a non-profit organization that serves veterans. summary: A Shungnak student tends the It’s a ‘We’ Community Garden, which provides learning opportunities and helps provide fresh vegetables for the village.
